chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْمَحْصُورِ وَ الْمَصْدُودِ وَ مَا عَلَيْهِمَا مِنَ الْكَفَّارَةِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 101 – The confined one and the prevented one, what is the expiation upon the both of them

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/343)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/343)
text: الْمَصْدُودُ يَذْبَحُ حَيْثُ صُدَّ وَ يَرْجِعُ صَاحِبُهُ فَيَأْتِي النِّسَاءَ وَ الْمَحْصُورُ يَبْعَثُ بِهَدْيِهِ وَ يَعِدُهُمْ يَوْماً فَإِذَا بَلَغَ الْهَدْيُ أَحَلَّ هَذَا فِي مَكَانِهِ

قُلْتُ لَهُ أَ رَأَيْتَ إِنْ رَدُّوا عَلَيْهِ دَرَاهِمَهُ وَ لَمْ يَذْبَحُوا عَنْهُ وَ قَدْ أَحَلَّ فَأَتَى النِّسَاءَ قَالَ فَلْيُعِدْ وَ لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ شَيْ‏ءٌ وَ لْيُمْسِكِ الْآنَ عَنِ النِّسَاءِ إِذَا بَعَثَ .

en.hubeali: Humeyd Bin Ziyad, from Al Hassan Bin Muhammad Bin Sama’at, from Ahmad Bin Al Hassan Al Maysami, from Aban, from Zurara,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’farasws having said: ‘The prevented one should slaughter (his sacrificial animal) where he has been prevented, and he would return, and he can go to the women. And the one confined (by an illness) should send his sacrificial animal, and they would appoint a day. So when the sacrificial animal reaches (its destination), this one would free himself from his Ihraam in his place’.

I said to himasws, ‘What is yourasws view if they were to return the Dirham back to him and they do not slaughter on his behalf, and he has already freed himself from his Ihraam, and has gone to the women?’ Heasws said: ‘So let him repeat, and there would be nothing (expiation) upon, and let him refrain from the women now, when he sends (his sacrificial animal)’.
en.sarwar: 9. Al-Nadr ibn Suwayd has narrated from al-Hassan ibn Muhammad ibn Sama‘ah from Ahmad ibn al-Hassan al-Mithamiy from Aban from Zurarah who has said the following: “Abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Al-Masdud (prevented) offers an animal for slaughtering wherever he is prevented and returns home. Women are lawful for him. Al-Mahsur (confined) sends his offering, sets up a certain time and when the offering reaches its place, he in his place comes out of the state of Ihram.’ I then asked, ‘What happens if they returned dirham and did not slaughter, but he has come out of the state of Ihram and has gone with women? He (the Imam) said, ‘He must do it again; there is nothing on him but for now he must stay away from women when he sends his offering.’”
